---
title: "SeeNote"
subtitle: "Annotate audio and video to build ML training datasets"
---

This protocol provides comprehensive instructions for annotating audio
events in data used for buzzdetect model training and testing.

::: {.callout-note}
The canonical version of this protocol is maintained in
[Google Docs](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Efwxs4nEFczTx0Xw2-RcCwnpCOO7R367Cn1A0w47rZU/edit?usp=sharing).
:::

## Software setup

### Installing SeeNote

Navigate to [the SeeNote repository on GitHub](https://github.com/LukeHearon/SeeNote)
and download using the installation buttons in the Install section. Or, use the
following links:

- [macOS](https://github.com/LukeHearon/SeeNote/releases/latest/download/SeeNote-macOS.dmg)
- [Windows](https://github.com/LukeHearon/SeeNote/releases/latest/download/SeeNote-Windows.exe)
- [Linux](https://github.com/LukeHearon/SeeNote/releases/latest/download/SeeNote-Linux.AppImage)

On macOS, you'll need to walk through the [macOS woes](#macos-woes) section to
allow the program to run.

### macOS woes {#macos-woes}

::: {.callout-warning}
This section needs to be written. The original link pointed at the Linux
AppImage by mistake. Document the Gatekeeper / "app is damaged" workaround here.
:::

## Where to next

- [Orientation](orientation.qmd) — projects, the annotation screen, and the tools palette.
- [Annotation Workflow](annotation-workflow.qmd) — the step-by-step labeling process.
- [Tips for Annotating](tips-for-annotating.qmd) — what we label and what we don't.
- [Classification Dictionary](classification-dictionary.qmd) — the full buzzdetect class list.
